body:
DIRECTIVE: SLS(22) -- THE TWO BUILDABLE EXTRACTIONS. Structured build order; read fully before writing any code.

== MISSION CONTEXT ==
SLS(21) closed the implementation-correctness gap. Analysis of the completed phase suite identified exactly two remaining KNOWABLE-IN-ADVANCE extractions the system does not make: scheduled-event awareness and execution-regime awareness. Everything else missing is either irreducible uncertainty (managed by risk architecture, not code) or methodology redesign (explicitly deferred). These two modules are therefore the final buildable capability gaps. Build both into SLS(21) -> increment to SLS(22).

== HARD CONSTRAINTS (apply to every line) ==
C1. Base file: TUNGSTEN_9.43_A7.5_QUANT_SLS(21).mq4 (your build). No other base.
C2. No live chart attach at any point. Compile gate per module: 0 errors before the next module begins.
C3. Minimal blast radius: new subsystems are additive layers with single, clearly-marked integration points into existing decision paths. No restructuring of existing logic.
C4. Persistence follows the established guarded-marker precedent (0xC59A, 0xD520): new tails only, FileIsEnding-guarded reads, older files degrade safely. SealArmedSave stays tail-free per precedent.
C5. Silent-mode discipline: every g_CalibSilent / logging conditional MUST use explicit braces. The dangling-if class killed three calibration phases; it does not get a fourth victim.
C6. Every new decision the system makes must be observable: log state TRANSITIONS (never per-tick spam), one-line daily summaries, and inline SLS22 markers on every integration point.
C7. Fail-open honestly: if either module's data source is missing or stale, the EA trades WITHOUT that gate exactly as SLS(21) does today, logs one WARN at init plus one per session, and surfaces the degraded state in MarketAwarenessState. Silent degradation is forbidden.

== MODULE 1: SCHEDULED-EVENT GATE (SEG) ==
Purpose: the one market discontinuity published in advance -- known economic events -- currently invisible to the system. Sessions know hours; SEG knows catalysts.

M1.1 DATA ARCHITECTURE (creative solution -- you are the feed):
MQL4 has no native calendar API. You, TCA, become the pipeline: create a companion skill + scheduled task that regenerates MQL4/Files/TUNGSTEN_events.csv weekly (and on demand) from public economic-calendar data via your own web access. CSV schema, one event per line:
  utc_timestamp;currency;impact(1=low,2=med,3=high);name
Include at minimum: NFP, US CPI, FOMC rate decisions + minutes, BoJ policy decisions, US GDP, PCE, JOLTS, ISM -- anything USD or JPY impact>=2 within the next 14 days. Write atomically (temp file + rename) so the EA never reads a half-written file.

M1.2 EA-SIDE CONSUMPTION:
- Load at OnInit; re-check file modification time hourly, reload on change.
- Staleness rule: newest event timestamp older than NOW, or file older than 7 days -> stale -> fail-open per C7.
- In-memory: sorted array of upcoming events for the chart symbol's two currencies only.

M1.3 GATE LOGIC (tiered by impact, minutes configurable as inputs with these defaults):
  impact 3: block new entries T-30min before -> T+15min after; existing positions: tighten SL to breakeven-or-better where price allows at T-10min. Never widen, never market-close solely for the event.
  impact 2: block new entries T-10min -> T+5min. No position modification.
  impact 1: no blocking; annotate logs only.
- Post-event release: re-entry additionally requires ERM (Module 2) reporting NORMAL -- the two modules interlock here; if ERM absent/stale, fall back to fixed T+ windows alone.
- Integration point: single gate check at the TOP of the entry-decision path, before edge validation spends any computation. One integration point, marked SLS22-SEG.

M1.4 STATE + OBSERVABILITY:
- New fields in MarketAwarenessState: nextEventTime, nextEventImpact, eventGateActive, eventFeedFresh.
- Log entries: gate ARM (event name, impact, window), gate RELEASE, feed reload, staleness WARN.

== MODULE 2: EXECUTION-REGIME MONITOR (ERM) ==
Purpose: ghost validation proves the logic on historical bars; it cannot see spread widening, slippage, or requotes. ERM measures whether the live plumbing still resembles the assumptions the edge was validated under -- and throttles when it does not.

M2.1 MEASUREMENT (rolling, EWMA alpha=0.05 unless justified otherwise):
- Realized spread: sampled at every entry-decision evaluation (not every tick).
- Slippage: |requested - OrderOpenPrice()| in points, captured at every fill inside SafeOrderSend's success path.
- Rejection rate: requotes + relevant error codes per attempt, same capture point.
- Rollover awareness: maintain a separate spread EWMA for the 21:00-23:00 UTC window vs the rest -- rollover widening is normal, not hostile; the two baselines prevent false HOSTILE states nightly.

M2.2 BASELINE + CLASSIFICATION (three states with hysteresis):
- Baseline spread = calibration-time assumed spread if recorded, else the long EWMA itself after a 200-sample warmup (state UNKNOWN until warmed; UNKNOWN behaves as NORMAL but is logged distinctly).
- NORMAL: realized within 1.5x applicable baseline and slippage EWMA <= configured tolerance.
- DEGRADED: spread > 1.5x baseline OR slippage EWMA > tolerance -> halve position size, skip signals whose edge margin is below median (consume the existing edge-strength value; do not invent a new metric).
- HOSTILE: spread > 2.5x baseline OR rejection rate > 20% over last 20 attempts -> no new entries, manage-only.
- Hysteresis: state upgrades require 5 consecutive qualifying samples; downgrades require 15. No flapping.
- Integration points: size multiplier into the existing final-size computation; entry permission alongside SEG's gate. Both marked SLS22-ERM.

M2.3 REALIZED-COST FEEDBACK:
Accumulate realized cost (spread + slippage) per closed trade as an average points-per-trade haircut. Expose it via one function; wire it as an input into the existing unified growth-rate core (CalculateDefensibleDailyGrowthRateCore) so the derived growth rate prices in real friction, not assumed friction. Single wiring point, marked.

M2.4 PERSISTENCE: new guarded tail, marker 0xE210: both spread EWMAs, slippage EWMA, sample counts, avg cost haircut, state. Follows C4 exactly.

M2.5 OBSERVABILITY: state-transition logs with the triggering measurements; one daily summary line (avg spread vs baseline, avg slippage, rejection %, current state, cost haircut).

== SEQUENCING & GATES ==
S1. Build SEG -> compile 0 errors -> relay checkpoint report.
S2. Build ERM -> compile 0 errors -> relay checkpoint report.
S3. Identity: SLS(21) -> SLS(22) in all identity contexts (header, banners, init strings); historical fix-tags untouched.
S4. THEN execute the still-open verification chain from d3e1191e ON SLS(22): invalidate saved state -> full fresh calibration (Phases 1-6) -> ghost validation to verdict. One calibration validates everything: the 30 accumulated fixes, the newly-awakened phases and loss-learning, and both new modules. Quantify the midrank calibration speedup vs prior runs while you are there.
S5. Deliver: SLS(22) .mq4 + manifest into files/from-agent; consolidated relay report: compile results, calibration duration + IC deltas, ghost verdict (WR/PF/trades/gate), derived growth rate with its inputs INCLUDING the new cost haircut, SEG feed status, ERM warmup state.

== EXPLICIT EXCLUSIONS ==
No methodology redesign. No live attach (ghost only; live requires Manuel's explicit go-ahead). No new indicators or signal components. No touching regimeStrategies[9-11].

Execute autonomously through S5. Checkpoint reports at S1, S2, and S5 minimum.
